contextRules.description: Per-persona game outcome, the closest existing shared facet for game success rate.

outcome_status.description: eliminated: persona.eliminated == true in final_state. survived: eliminated == false and the persona appears in at least one battle_resolved event in game_log.json.events. bystander: eliminated == false and the persona never appears in a battle_resolved event (timed out without ever finishing a duel).

final_stars.description: final_state.<persona_id>.stars at the end of the run.

contextRules.description: Per-persona free-text reasoning attached to decisions over the course of a run, grouped by trait so reasoning style can be compared across personas and across sessions with different crew_manifest.yaml trait mixes.

dominant_trait.description: game_log.json.personas[*].traits_summary.dominant_trait, the trait dimension brains.py already keys challenge-probability bias off of.

reasoning_events.description: The optional free-text 'reasoning' field on game_log.json.events entries (say/move/challenge/play_card/private_message/battle_resolved, etc.) where a persona's brain supplied one. Not every event carries this field.

summaryDirectives.description: Summarize each persona's reasoning trajectory text, grouped by dominant_trait, to compare how reasoning style differs across trait groups within and across sessions.

judgeDirectives.description: Score the persona's reasoning trajectory text for confusion (did the persona seem lost or contradictory about the game state), funness, and engagement (did the persona seem invested in the moment-to-moment play). This is a starting rubric set, not exhaustive -- some funness/engagement/confusion signals are inherently game-dependent (e.g. specific to duels, proximity-seeking, or bluffing) and should be extended per-game rather than assumed to transfer as-is to other game tasks.
